channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Americans skeptical of the Iran war, poll says. And, DOJ gives guns back to felons
item.description: A majority of Americans oppose the U.S.' involvement in the war with Iran, according to a new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll. And, the Department of Justice is quietly restoring gun rights to felons.
item.content:encoded:
A majority of Americans oppose the U.S.' involvement in the war with Iran, according to a new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll. And, the Department of Justice is quietly restoring gun rights to felons.

(Image credit: Christopher Furlong)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: U.S. attacks Iranian mine-laying vessels near Hormuz on Day 12 of war
item.description: Attacks and counterattacks continued throughout the Middle East Wednesday. Two cargo ships were struck in the Gulf, as some lawmakers in Washington pressed for answers on the war's rationale.
item.content:encoded:
Attacks and counterattacks continued throughout the Middle East Wednesday. Two cargo ships were struck in the Gulf, as some lawmakers in Washington pressed for answers on the war's rationale.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Over puppy yoga? Try it with snakes.
item.description: You've heard of yoga with kittens, and goats, and maybe even reindeer… but what about a bunch of pythons and one baby Columbian Common Boa named Mango?
item.content:encoded:
You've heard of yoga with kittens, and goats, and maybe even reindeer… but what about a bunch of pythons and one baby Columbian Common Boa named Mango?

(Image credit: Celeste Noche for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Deena Prichep

item.title: Americans are split on wanting the National Guard to monitor voting, a new poll finds
item.description: Nearly half of Americans support the National Guard monitoring November's elections, potentially signaling an openness to the sort of nationalizing of elections that President Trump says he wants.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly half of Americans support the National Guard monitoring November's elections, potentially signaling an openness to the sort of nationalizing of elections that President Trump says he wants.

(Image credit: Leonardo Munoz)
item.dc:creator: Miles Parks

item.title: The Trump DOJ is giving guns back to felons, including one alleged fake elector
item.description: The Department of Justice is quietly restarting a decades-dormant program to restore gun rights to felons. One of them was an alleged fake elector in 2020.
item.content:encoded:
The Department of Justice is quietly restarting a decades-dormant program to restore gun rights to felons. One of them was an alleged fake elector in 2020.

(Image credit: Ted S. Warren)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Key oversight helping keep student loan records accurate has stopped, a watchdog says
item.description: Without this Education Department oversight, borrowers could "be placed in the wrong loan repayment status, billed for incorrect amounts" and more, the U.S. Government Accountability Office says.
item.content:encoded: Without this Education Department oversight, borrowers could "be placed in the wrong loan repayment status, billed for incorrect amounts" and more, the U.S. Government Accountability Office says.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: 2025 saw relatively fewer natural disasters. Will you get a break on home insurance?
item.description: Disaster costs fell in the U.S. in 2025. Still, it was the fourth time in five years that extreme weather inflicted more than $100 billion in annual losses. Industry experts say the growing financial toll will make insurers wary of rushing to cut rates.
item.content:encoded:
Disaster costs fell in the U.S. in 2025. Still, it was the fourth time in five years that extreme weather inflicted more than $100 billion in annual losses. Industry experts say the growing financial toll will make insurers wary of rushing to cut rates.

(Image credit: ALLISON JOYCE/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: Bam! Heat's Adebayo scores 83 points, 2nd only to Wilt Chamberlain in NBA history
item.description: Bam Adebayo had a night for all time on Tuesday, with a point total second to only Wilt Chamberlain in the NBA record books.
item.content:encoded:
Bam Adebayo had a night for all time on Tuesday, with a point total second to only Wilt Chamberlain in the NBA record books.

(Image credit: Rebecca Blackwell)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Prosecutor says Rihanna, family were home when woman charged with attempted murder fired
item.description: Rihanna, her partner A$AP Rocky, their three children and her mother were all at home when a woman now charged with attempted murder is alleged to have fired at the property, a prosecutor said.
item.content:encoded:
Rihanna, her partner A$AP Rocky, their three children and her mother were all at home when a woman now charged with attempted murder is alleged to have fired at the property, a prosecutor said.

(Image credit: Damian Dovarganes)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Senate Democrats ramp up pressure campaign for public hearings on war with Iran
item.description: Congressional Democrats are demanding transparency in the form of public hearings from Trump administration officials on the timeline and objectives of the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded:
Congressional Democrats are demanding transparency in the form of public hearings from Trump administration officials on the timeline and objectives of the war in Iran.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt
